authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 20000-1:2018 Information technology - Service management - Part 1: Service management system requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: International Organization for Standardization and International Electrotechnical Commission
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO/IEC 20000-1 is a recognized service management system standard for technology service planning, delivery, monitoring, review, and improvement, directly grounding platform support request, incident, service-level, and documentation workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service intake, status validation, service-level handling, support disposition, and continual-improvement handoff
used_for: service request, incident, and problem workflow vocabulary

authoritative_sources.title: ITIL 4 service management framework
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ITIL is a recognized service-management framework used across software and information-service organizations for service desk, incident, service request, problem, knowledge, change, and continual-improvement practices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service desk classification, runbook use, incident/request distinction, knowledge use, and escalation
used_for: separating incidents, service requests, known errors, problems, and changes
knowledge article and approved communication discipline
handoff to engineering, product, incident, and change owners

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 10002:2018 Quality management - Customer satisfaction - Guidelines for complaints handling in organizations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 10002 is a recognized complaints-handling standard that supports objective complaint intake, response, communication, review, and improvement expectations for customer-facing platform support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Complaint intake, response, repeat-failure handling, quality review, and escalation
used_for: repeat failure and service recovery escalation

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-61 Rev. 3, Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations for Cybersecurity Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-61r3 is official U.S. government cybersecurity incident-response guidance and is authoritative for identifying when a support request involving suspected compromise, data exposure, or security harm must move to incident response.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Security-sensitive classification, containment of support messaging, and incident-owner escalation
used_for: evidence preservation and approved communication constraints

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Privacy Framework
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST's Privacy Framework is an official privacy-risk management framework used to define privacy and confidentiality constraints when platform support handles customer, subscriber, or tenant data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Confidentiality screening, privacy-rights routing, data-minimization, and approved disclosure
used_for: customer and tenant data handling constraints
recipient, channel, and disclosure refusal behavior

authoritative_sources.title: Protecting Personal Information: A Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the U.S. consumer-protection regulator and its business guidance is authoritative for personal-information protection expectations relevant to support access, disclosure, retention, and escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Customer data handling, controlled communication, and unsafe disclosure refusal
used_for: data minimization and secure handling expectations
unapproved recipient or channel refusal triggers

authoritative_sources.title: Section 512 of Title 17: Resources on Online Service Provider Safe Harbors and Notice-and-Takedown
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The U.S. Copyright Office provides official resources on online service provider notice-and-takedown and counter-notice processes, making it authoritative for digital media content workflow boundaries in platform support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Content workflow status handling, takedown/counter-notice boundary screening, and legal-owner handoff
used_for: content asset evidence and approval requirements

authoritative_sources.title: 15-1232.00 Computer User Support Specialists
authoritative_sources.publisher: O*NET OnLine, sponsored by the U.S. Department of Labor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: O*NET is sponsored by the U.S. Department of Labor and provides task-level public references for user support, troubleshooting, documentation, answering inquiries, and referring unresolved technology issues.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Troubleshooting intake, customer communication, recordkeeping, and unresolved issue handoff

authoritative_sources.title: Trust Services Criteria for Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA trust services criteria are a recognized service-organization control framework for security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy, supporting platform-support caveats around availability, data handling, and evidence claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Availability, confidentiality, privacy, and processing-integrity claim boundary handling

workflow_stages.name: Support intake and classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm customer or stakeholder identity, tenant, product or content area, request channel, issue type, service tier, entitlement, business impact, and case identifiers before classifying the request as informational, exception-driven, complaint-driven, owner-transfer sensitive, blocked, or in-scope.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Record-of-truth and prerequisite retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Verify CRM, ticketing, account, subscription, entitlement, content, incident, status-page, project, document, approval, and audit records before stating status, applying a runbook, or sending customer-facing communication.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Supported troubleshooting or fulfillment
workflow_stages.description: Apply approved knowledge articles, runbooks, service catalog rules, content workflow facts, incident updates, and documented service policies while avoiding unapproved production fixes, privacy-rights determinations, refund promises, or legal/editorial judgments.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Customer and stakeholder communication
workflow_stages.description: Prepare approved, evidence-backed communication that states request summary, action taken, current status, unresolved items, next owner, and caveats without overstating root cause, uptime, content, legal, privacy, compensation, roadmap, or contractual authority.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Resolution confirmation and case disposition
workflow_stages.description: Close or hold the request only when the required case record, source-system evidence, customer confirmation requirement, communication log, content or incident state, and unresolved-item routing support the disposition.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, complaint, repeat-failure, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Create an escalation package when the request asks for unsupported workarounds, policy exceptions, credits, compensation, content decisions, privacy-rights handling, security incident decisions, legal/editorial/contractual signoff, or cross-source conflict resolution.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: Support tickets, CRM cases, chat/email/call transcripts, communication logs, and customer complaint records
Tenant service catalog, support plan, subscription or entitlement records, customer account records, SLA or response targets, and escalation matrix
Knowledge-base articles, troubleshooting runbooks, incident updates, status-page records, release notes, known-issue records, and approved customer messaging
Product telemetry references, error IDs, audit logs, access logs, screenshots, reproduction notes, configuration prerequisites, and diagnostic summaries
Content asset IDs, content management records, metadata, publishing queue states, moderation or review notes, copyright notice or counter-notice workflow status, and editorial approval records
Project plans, delivery records, client commitments, contractual evidence, approval notes, quality checks, review notes, and service-recovery records
Escalation packets for unsupported workarounds, repeat failures, policy exceptions, confidentiality conflicts, privacy or security concerns, content workflow blockers, and authority-sensitive asks
systems_of_record: CRM, case-management, and customer communication systems
Ticketing, service desk, incident, problem, and knowledge-management systems
Subscription, billing, entitlement, account, identity, and tenant administration systems
Product telemetry, observability, audit-log, access-log, status-page, and release-management systems
Content management, digital asset management, publishing, moderation, review, and localization systems
Document management, project management, delivery, quality-review, and approval-routing systems
Privacy, security, retention, legal hold, and compliance evidence systems

allowed: Resolve supported platform requests when tenant service policy, customer identity, entitlement, product or content context, source records, and approved runbook or messaging support the response.
Request minimum missing facts and authoritative records needed to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, customer or stakeholder communication summaries, request-resolution summaries, and escalation packets without overstating authority.

Route the case to the correct adjacent owner when the workflow becomes legal, editorial, privacy, security incident, product, engineering, billing, refund, delivery, account, or contractual approval work.
escalate: Required tenant facts, product area, entitlement, content lifecycle state, source-system record, approved messaging, contract term, or approval owner is missing or contradictory.

The request seeks a policy exception, unsupported workaround, refund, service credit, compensation, SLA exception, content exception, privacy-rights determination, or customer-harm decision beyond documented authority.

Legal interpretation, editorial judgment, copyright, privacy rights, security incident classification, professional judgment, production engineering, product roadmap, billing approval, or contractual signoff determines the answer.
Public standards, tenant policies, contract terms, incident communications, content workflow rules, or customer commitments materially conflict.
refuse: Requests for legal, editorial, privacy-rights, copyright, security-incident-command, engineering, product-roadmap, refund, compensation, or contractual approval without explicit authority.
Requests to fabricate evidence, bypass approvals, suppress audit or communication records, disclose confidential records to unapproved parties, or claim unsupported resolution status.
Requests to bypass documented support, entitlement, pricing, eligibility, content, privacy, security, retention, quality-review, or contractual controls.

domain_failure_modes: Closing a platform support ticket as resolved when entitlement records, incident status, runbook evidence, or customer confirmation requirements are missing.
Treating customer support as authority to approve refunds, service credits, compensation, SLA exceptions, content removal, publication, privacy-rights requests, or contractual commitments.
Disclosing customer, tenant, content, security, incident, or confidential business records to an unapproved recipient or channel.
Using generic customer-service language that hides product area, tenant scope, subscription tier, source-system evidence, incident caveats, or content lifecycle status.
Answering legal, editorial, copyright, privacy, security incident, engineering, product roadmap, or contractual signoff questions instead of handing off with the evidence package.
Closing repeat incidents or complaint-driven cases without preserving issue history, impact, prior actions, owner routing, and service-recovery constraints.
Representing status-page facts, incident root cause, uptime commitment, release timing, or content moderation outcome beyond approved support messaging.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific authority thresholds for refunds, service credits, SLA exceptions, content exceptions, and customer-impact communications
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Treat missing thresholds as escalation-only and do not imply approval, credit, compensation, or exception authority.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether a tenant routes content workflow exceptions first to legal, editorial, trust-and-safety, content operations, or account management
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require tenant owner map at runtime and return orchestrator-safe routing when the owner is unspecified.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific privacy, copyright, consumer-protection, and contractual commitments may be stricter than the public baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require tenant jurisdiction, policy set, contract terms, and approved messaging at runtime; use stricter tenant controls until reviewed.
